title: 0/1 Knapsack Problem
summary: Pack items into a knapsack without exceeding capacity, maximizing total value.
problem_statement: I have 5 items with weights [2, 3, 4, 5, 9] and values [3, 4, 5, 8, 10]. My knapsack can hold at most 20 units of weight. Which items should I pack to maximize total value?
real_world_example: A delivery driver deciding which packages to load given a vehicle weight limit, or a hiker choosing supplies for a trip with a backpack capacity.

expected_pattern.variables: x_i in {0, 1} — whether item i is selected

expected_solution_summary: Items 0, 2, 3, 4 are packed (total weight 20, total value 26).
learning_notes: The canonical 'select a subset under a budget' QUBO. The capacity constraint becomes a Lagrange penalty in the BQM. You'll see this same shape in: portfolio selection, feature selection, resource allocation.

learning_objectives: Translate a verbal capacity constraint into a Lagrange penalty term
Compare exact and heuristic solver behavior on a single small problem
